title: festuca wagneri var. hungarica
intro: Festuca wagneri var. hungarica, also known as Hungarian fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing pain, swelling, and fever. The health benefits of Festuca wagneri var. hungarica include its ability to soothe digestive issues, calm respiratory problems, and even lower blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used topically as a poultice or salve to aid in wound healing and skin issues.
